observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and the
Municipal Treasurer (MT) to verify with the Provincial Accountant and Provincial
Treasurer the discrepancies of P31,069.81 and P32,974.29 between the recorded
balances of the reciprocal accounts reflected in the books of the Province and
adjust the same to present the correct balances of the said accounts in the
Municipality’s financial statement.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. Municipal Treasurer to intensify collections of the Loans Receivable, designate some key personnel who will monitor the loans granted and enforce payment thereof and institute legal actions against debtors; and
b. Municipal Accountant to send demand letters to concerned debtors to help enforce collections.
observations.managementAction:
Debtors were already paying by installments.
The Municipal Treasurer sent demand letter to the debtors.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and the Provincial Treasurer to verify the discrepancies of P102,116.13 and P163,430.54 between the recorded balances of Due from and Due to LGUs reflected in the books of the Province and adjust the same to present the correct balances of the said accounts in the Municipality’s financial statement.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant is always sending emails to the Provincial Accounting Office for the reconciliation process.
observations.reason: Reconciliation between Provincial Accounting Office and the Municipal Accounting Office is still on-going as of year-end.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Assessor continue exerting efforts in securing all requirements/documents needed for the titling of the said lots in the name of the Municipality to avoid third party claims in the future and possible land disputes.
observations.reason: The titling process is on-going as of year-end.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor oblige the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Budget Officer to continue their efforts in securing all legal documents necessary in determining the insurable amounts of the said properties and to make sure that all the insurable properties of the Municipality are covered with insurance with the GSIS.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality, being fifth class Municipality is exempted under RA No. 656.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Agriculturist to prioritize the construction of CFLC embankment to make it operational.
We further recommended that the Municipality create a committee that will supervise the operations and extend also all assistance needed in its initial operation to attain the objectives of the project for the benefit of the constituents.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipality constructed the embankment needed for the operation.
The committee was created to assist the operations and extend also all assistance needed in its initial operation to attain the objectives of the project.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the GSO/Appraisal and Disposal Committee to proceed with the appraisal and disposal of the unserviceable properties in accordance with Section 79 of P.D. No. 1445 to avoid further deterioration and earn at least the salvage value from the sale of these assets.
observations.managementAction: The designated GSO already in the process of preparing the IIRUP.
observations.reason: Completion of legal documents that are needed in public auction of the properties need to be secured first.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MT and Municipal Accountant to release the RPT share of the component Barangays promptly within five days after the end of each quarter in accordance with Section 36 of COA Circular No. 92-382 and Section 271 (d) of RA No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality distributed the RPT share of the component Barangays promptly within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to confirm with account owners/creditors and to request them to submit documents pertaining to their claims from the Municipality. In the event that they fail to support their claims, revert the account to the Unappropriated Surplus of the GF, subject to an authority from the Sangguniang Bayan through a Resolution with a condition that appropriation shall be made in the event that claim arises in the future, pursuant to the provisions of Section 98 of P.D. No. 1445.
observations.managementAction:
Sangguniang Bayan Resolution No. 2021-64 dated May 10, 2021 was approved for the reversion of AP to Unappropriated Surplus of GF.
Furthermore, the Municipal Accountant draw an accounting entry for the reversion under Journal Voucher Entry (JEV) No. 100-021-12-00059 dated December 31, 2021.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the General Services Officer (GSO) to ensure that Property Acknowledgment Receipt is renewed every three years or whenever there is a transfer of Property ownership and/or custodianship.
observations.managementAction: The PAR was updated and renewed by the GSO every time there is a transfer of property ownership and/or custodianship.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor:
a. coordinate with the Sangguniang Bayan with the assistance of the LGU GFPS, to develop and pass a GAD code to comply with the provision of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 to support the localization of Magna Carta of Women and which will serve as basis for identifying programs, activities and projects on GAD as prescribed in PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01;
b. maximize the GAD funds through the Joint implementation of GAD-related programs and projects in order to optimally utilize the GAD Budget; and
c. require the MPDC to expedite the completion of CBMS and institutionalize a GAD database with adequate sex disaggregated data.
observations.managementAction:
A GAD code was Passed by Sangguniang Bayan (SB) under SB resolution no. 2021-04 dated April 12, 2021.
The GAD Budget was optimally utilized as of year-end.
Completed a CMBS and GAD database with adequate sex disaggregated data.
observations.reason:
a. The Municipality was not able to develop and pass its GAD Code providing the bases for gender analysis, programming and policy formulation of the Municipality with the assistance of the Municipal GAD Focal Point System.
b. The amount appropriated for GAD programs, projects and activities in CY 2020 was not fully utilized, leaving an unexpended balance of P2,357,926.78 at year-end, thus precluding the intent of the program to pursue women’s empowerment and gender equality.
c. The Municipality has not established or set up its Gender and Development (GAD) database, as source of information for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation since it is still in the process of collecting sex-disaggregated data, as required under Section 4.1.B of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2013-01, thus there was no assurance that the GAD program and activities as planned and implemented during the year promoted gender-responsive governance as prescribed in the same JMC.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipality, through the National Solid Waste Management Commission (NSWMC) secure the approval of their 10-year SWMP and expedite the closure of their open dump site pursuant to RA No. 9003.
observations.managementAction:
Open dump site was already closed, however, the EMB is for submission to the concerned agency.
Also, we recommended to expedite the closure of the open dump site.
observations.reason: Awaiting for the approval of the SWMP.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor request the:
a) SB to authorize him to file the request for write-off of dormant receivables; and
b) Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to support the request for authority to write-off of dormant receivables following the guidelines and procedures provided under COA Circular No. 2016-005.
observations.managementAction:
Not Implemented
The SB did not grant the request for write-off of loans receivables due to possible negative impact on the existing livelihood Program.
(Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 1, Page No. 31)
The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to support the request for authority to write-off of dormant receivables. The Municipal Treasurer’s Office and Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office (MSWDO) will still exert more efforts in collecting the outstanding receivables.
Following the guidelines and procedures provided under COA Circular No. 2016-005.
observations.reason: The SB did not grant the request for write-off of loans receivables due to possible negative impact on the existing livelihood Program.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor to continue exerting efforts in securing all requirements/documents needed for the titling of the said lots in the name of the Municipality to avoid third party claims in the future and possible land disputes.
observations.managementAction:
Not Implemented
The Municipal Assessor will gather the required documents which pertains to the first lot where the Public Market/KALAHI Office was situated for filing of special application. As to the second lot where the Eco-Park / Dumpsite / Cemetery was located, it was informed that there was a conflict with the approved survey plan based on the assessment of DENR Regional Office and still waiting for their updates. On the other hand, the third lot where Senior Citizen’s Building is located, it was learned that the original land title was lost and it has been difficult for the current owners to register their own land titles. An assistance was asked from the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) to discuss the possible remedy.
(Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 3, Page No. 34)
observations.reason: No update; conflicts with approved survey plan per DENR assessment; missing land title for third lot; assistance asked from SB.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. Municipal Treasurer to intensify collections of the Loans Receivable, designate
some key personnel who will monitor the loans granted and enforce payment
thereof and to institute legal actions against debtors; and
b. Municipal Accountant to send demand letters to concerned debtors to help enforce
collections.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and the
Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the Provincial Accountant and Provincial
Treasurer to verify the discrepancies of P102,116.13 and P163,430.54 between the
recorded balances of Due from and Due to LGUs accounts of the Municipality and
their reciprocal accounts Due to and Due from LGUs reflected in the books of the
Province to present the correct balances of the said accounts in the Municipality’s
FS.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Assessor to continue exerting efforts in securing all requirements/documents
needed for the titling of the said lots in the name of the Municipality to avoid third
party claims in the future and possible land disputes.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor oblige the Municipal
Treasurer and Municipal Budget Officer to continue their efforts in securing all
legal documents necessary in determining the insurable amounts of the said
properties and to make sure that all the insurable properties of the Municipality are
covered with insurance with the GSIS.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Agriculturist to
prioritize the construction of embankment around the CFLC to make it operational.
We further recommended that the Municipality create a committee that will
supervise the operations and extend also all assistance needed in its initial
operation to attain the objectives of the project for the benefit of the constituents.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the
GSO/Appraisal and Disposal Committee to proceed with the appraisal and disposal
of the unserviceable properties in accordance with Section 79 of P.D. No. 1445 to
avoid further deterioration and earn at least the salvage value from the sale of these
assets.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MT and
Municipal Accountant to release the RPT share of the component Barangays
promptly within five days after the end of each quarter in accordance with Section
36 of COA Circular No. 92-382 and Section 271 (d) of RA No. 7160.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
confirm with account owners/creditors and to request them to submit documents
pertaining to their claims from the Municipality. In the event that they fail to support
their claims, revert the account to the Unappropriated Surplus of the GF, subject to an
authority from the Sangguniang Bayan through a Resolution with a condition that
appropriation shall be made in the event that claim arises in the future, pursuant to the
provisions of Section 98 of P.D. No. 1445.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the General
Services Officer to ensure that Property Acknowledgment Receipt is renewed every
three years or whenever there is a change in the custodianship, in compliance with
Section 492 of the GAAM, Volume I, in order that record of accountability for PPE is
updated and to easily determined who is the personnel accountable for that
property.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Budget Officer to secure all the requirements in determining the insurable amounts of the said properties and to make sure that all insurable properties of the Municipality are covered with insurance with the GSIS.
observations.managementAction:
Not Implemented
The Management was able to apply for its property insurance as reflected under Policy No. FI-GIG-GSISLUC-0001102 covering the period from November 26, 2020 to November 26, 2021 for the Municipal Building. However, other insurable properties were not yet insured due to incomplete legal documents and the adverse effect of COVID-19 in the Philippines that hamper its application for insurance.
(Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 4, Page No.35)
observations.reason: Other insurable properties were not yet insured due to incomplete legal documents and the adverse effect of COVID-19 that hampered application for insurance.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor coordinate with the SB in expediting the enactment of the ordinance that will govern the CFLC’s operations. We further recommended that the Municipality extend all assistance needed in its initial operation to attain the objectives of the project for the benefit of the constituents.
observations.managementAction:
Not Implemented
The Municipality passed an ordinance under SB Resolution No. 2020-08 dated September 8, 2020 to govern its operations. However, despite its approval, the Municipality deferred its implementation due to embankment of the CFLC site needed to make it operational and to serve the public better. The Municipality allocated P1,600,000.00 budget for its construction. The CFLC Project Committee has been created to supervise and implement its operation under Executive Order No. 13 series of 2021.
(Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 5, Page No. 36)
observations.reason: Deferred implementation due to embankment of the CFLC site needed to make it operational; project procurement in process.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the GSO/Appraisal and Disposal Committee to prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) and to facilitate the appraisal and disposal of the unserviceable properties in accordance with Section 79 of P.D. No. 1445 to avoid further deterioration and earn at least the salvage value from the sale of these assets.
observations.managementAction:
Not Implemented
A personnel has been assigned specifically to facilitate the completion of documentary requirements to ensure disposal of unserviceable properties by CY 2021. Moreover, the Appraisal and Disposal Committee submitted an Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Properties (IIRUP) to the Audit Team.
(Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 6, Page No.37)
observations.reason: Conflicting schedules of personnel delayed disposal; assignment of personnel and submission of IIRUP ongoing to ensure disposal by CY 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to release the RPT share of the component Barangays promptly within five days after the end of each quarter in accordance with Section 36 of COA Circular No. 92-382 and Section 271 (d) of RA No. 7160.
observations.managementAction:
Not Implemented
The Municipality explained that the five days reglementary period is short considering manual recording and when end of month falls on a Friday with limited working days, and that alternative work arrangements and skeletal schedules due to the COVID-19 pandemic affected compliance. However, for CY 2021 first quarter, the Municipality remitted the share of 14 Barangays on April 7, 2021 and committed to try to make it on the three succeeding quarters.
(Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 7, Page No. 38)
observations.reason: Manual recording, timing (end of quarter on holiday/Friday), and COVID-19 related work arrangements delayed timely reconciliation and remittance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor coordinate with the SB on the enactment of an ordinance that will create Economic Enterprises for the operation of market, cemetery, slaughterhouse and hostels/dormitories pursuant to DBM Local Budget Circular No. 111. Likewise, require the Municipal Accountant to prepare a separate FS for each economic enterprise so that adequate information as to assets, liabilities, equity and results of operations can be monitored and evaluated on a regular basis.
observations.managementAction:
Not Implemented
The Municipality informed the concerned personnel and the United Coconut Planters Bank regarding the matter. The Municipality submitted separate FS for the operations of their Public Market, cemetery, slaughterhouse and hostels/dormitories pursuant to DBM Local Budget Circular No. 111. As a result, adequate information on assets, liabilities, equity and results of operations can now be monitored and evaluated on a regular basis.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MHRMO to ensure that an employee's net take home pay shall not be less than P5,000.00 as provided under Section 52 of the GAA for FY 2019 by closely monitoring the loan applications of the employee.
observations.managementAction:
Not Implemented
The Municipality informed concerned personnel and the United Coconut Planters Bank regarding the matter. The Municipal HRMO will closely monitor loan applications to ensure the employee's net take home pay shall not be less than P5,000.00.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GSO-Designate to ensure that PAR is renewed every three years or whenever there is a change in the custodianship, in compliance with Section 492 of the GAAM, Volume I, in order that record of accountability for PPE is updated and to easily determined who is the personnel accountable for that property.
observations.managementAction:
Not Implemented
Updating of PAR has been suspended due to COVID-19 pandemic. In order to comply, a new personnel have been assigned to complete the updating by year 2021.
(Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 9, Page No. 41)
observations.reason: Updating suspended due to COVID-19; new personnel assigned to complete updating by 2021.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor and require the Municipal Treasurer to remit the unexpended balances of Due to NGAs to their Source Agencies/National Treasury so that funds may be utilized on other vital projects/programs.
observations.managementAction:
Not Implemented
The Municipality informed that they remitted the unexpended balances to their respective source agencies. (No further detail provided in the table.)

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to: a) Revert back to the General Fund (GF) the unexpended LDRRMF in CY 2014 since the five-year period has already lapsed; and b) Transfer to the Trust Fund books the unobligated/unexpended balance of the LDRRMF in CY 2019 to ensure that the LDRRMF will be used only for its intended purpose.
observations.managementAction:
Not Implemented
Reverted to GF. Transferred to TF books.
The Municipality reverted the unexpended LDRRMF in CY 2014 back to the General Fund since the five-year retention period lapsed and transferred the unobligated/unexpended balance of the LDRRMF in CY 2019 to the Trust Fund books to ensure proper utilization.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Disaster Risk Management Officer to fully implement the programmed projects in order to strengthen the capacity of the Municipality for disaster risk reduction and management.
observations.managementAction:
Not Implemented
The Municipality reiterated that they diverted fund for MDRRMO projects to COVID-19 related activities to address the impact of the pandemic in accordance with guidelines issued. They noted that they fully implemented projects/activities but diverted some MDRRMO projects to address COVID-19.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor: a) Enact an ordinance for the creation of a separate and independent LDRRMO; and b) Instruct the Head of the Human Resource Management Office (HRMO) to immediately fill-up the three mandatory positions of the LDRRMO as required under Section 5.2 of the NDRRMC, DILG, DBM, and CSC JMC.
observations.managementAction:
Not Implemented
Created the LDRRMO as per SB Resolution No. 2020-008. Mandatory positions of the LDRRMO were filled-up.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor: a) coordinate with the Sangguniang Bayan with the assistance of the LGU GFPS, to develop and pass a GAD code to comply with the provision of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 to support the localization of Magna Carta for Women and to ensure that GAD is mainstreamed in local policy-making and integrated in local development plans; b) ensure the maximize utilization of the GAD funds through the implementation of GAD-related programs and projects in order to attain the objective for which funds were provided; and c) require the MPDC to expedite the completion of CBMS and institutionalize a GAD database with adequate and systematically gathered sex-disaggregated data providing the bases for gender analysis, planning, programming and policy formulation of the Municipality with the assistance of the Municipal GAD Focal Point System.
observations.managementAction:
Not Implemented
The Municipality agreed and reported that SB passed Municipal Ordinance No. 2021-04 "AN ORDINANCE PROVIDING FOR A MUNICIPAL GENDER AND DEVELOPMENT CODE AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES" last April 16, 2021. The Municipality also noted that due to COVID-19 many PPAs related to GAD were not implemented leaving an unexpended balance of P2,357,926.78. Rapid CBMS was completed and processed data are now being utilized; processed, community validated and corrected data are now used for evidence-based planning and the MPDO maintains a GAD database.
(Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 10, Page No. 42)
observations.reason: PPAs not implemented due to COVID-19 pandemic resulting in unexpended GAD funds; CBMS completion delayed but later reported completed and GAD database institutionalized.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipality, through the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board, work for the completion of their 10-year SWMP and secure the approval of the plan from the NSWMC. Also, we recommended to expedite the closure of their open dump site pursuant to RA No. 9003.
observations.managementAction:
Not Implemented
The Municipality explained that the Safe Closure and Rehabilitation Plan (SCRP) is still in the process of completion. The remaining unutilized fund in the SCRP amounting to P123,820.93 were re-programmed thru coordination with the Environmental Management Bureau (EMB) intended for rental of equipment, procurement of additional soil cover, fuel, labor, materials and miscellaneous expenses. The Municipality issued a "Commitment Letter" last March 16, 2021 to the Regional Director of EMB to finally close the "Open Dump Site" until April 30, 2021 or 20 days after the approval for the re-programming of the remaining fund. They coordinated with the service provider to assist completion of the 10-year SWMP. Municipality amenable with recommendations.
(Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 11, Page No. 46)
observations.reason: SWMP drafting and safe closure plan still in process; closure pending re-programming approval and completion of SCRP activities; COVID-19 impacted timelines.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to remit the unremitted amount to the concerned agencies so that fund may be utilized on their priority projects/programs.
observations.managementAction:
Not Implemented
Remitted the amounts due to BIR and PhilHealth amounting to P53,113.88 and P8,709.09, respectively.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to oblige the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Budget Officer to secure all the requirements in determining the insurable amounts of the said properties and to make sure that all insurable properties of the Municipality are covered with insurance with the GSIS.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer is still in the process of completing the relevant documents needed in the application and processing of insurance of all properties owned by the LGU. (Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 3, Page No. 33)

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality extend all assistance needed in its initial operation to attain the objectives of the project for the benefit of the constituents. We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor coordinate with the Sangguniang Bayan in expediting the enactment of the ordinance that will govern the CFLC’s operations.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor is amenable with the audit observation and confirmed that the proposed ordinance that will govern the operation of the CFLC was still on its second reading. He explained further that the present committee is working on a much better version of the ordinance taking into consideration the request of the fisher folk among other salient features, for the improvement of the site where the building was constructed.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor to continue exerting efforts in securing all requirements/documents needed for the titling of the said lots in the name of the Municipality to avoid third party claims in the future and possible land disputes.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Assessor submitted the documents of Gymnasium lot to the Registry of Deeds and is awaiting for its approval. Open Dumpsite in Brgy. Sangirin has boundary dispute and the request was submitted already to DENR Regional IV-A. Still in the process of locating the title of other three lots. (Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 2, Page No. 31)

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor request the:
a) SB to authorize him to file the request for write-off of dormant receivables; and
b) Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to support the request for
authority to write-off dormant receivables following the guidelines and
procedures provided under COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Assessor to continue exerting efforts in securing all requirements/documents
needed for the titling of the said lots in the name of the Municipality to avoid third
party claims in the future and possible land disputes.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor oblige the Municipal
Treasurer and Municipal Budget Officer to continue their efforts in securing all legal
documents necessary in determining the insurable amounts of the said properties
and to make sure that all the insurable properties of the Municipality are covered
with insurance with the GSIS.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor coordinate with the SB
in expediting the enactment of the ordinance that will govern the CFLC’s
operations. We further recommended that the Municipality extend all assistance
needed in its initial operation to attain the objectives of the project for the benefit
of the constituents.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the
GSO/Appraisal and Disposal Committee to prepare the IIRUP and to facilitate the
appraisal and disposal of the unserviceable properties in accordance with Section
79 of P.D. No. 1445 to avoid further deterioration and earn at least the salvage value
from the sale of these assets.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer and
Municipal Accountant to release the RPT share of the component Barangays
promptly within five days after the end of each quarter in accordance with Section
36 of COA Circular No. 92-382 and Section 271 (d) of RA No. 7160.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor coordinate with the SB on the
enactment of an ordinance that will create Economic Enterprises for the operation
of market, cemetery, slaughterhouse and hostels/dormitories pursuant to DBM
Local Budget Circular No. 111. Likewise, require the Municipal Accountant to
prepare a separate FS for each economic enterprise so that adequate information
as to assets, liabilities, equity and results of operations can be monitored and
evaluated on a regular basis.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MHRMO to ensure that an
employee’s net take home pay shall not be less than the allowable take home pay of
P5,000.00 as provided under Section 52 of GAA for FY 2019 by closely monitoring the
loan applications of the employee.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GSO-Designate to ensure that
PAR is renewed every three years or whenever there is a change in the custodianship,
in compliance with Section 492 of the Government Accounting and Auditing Manual
(GAAM), Volume I, in order that record of accountability for PPE is updated.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the General Services Officer to determine the value of all the PPE items in the RPCPPE and present the said Report per type of PPE as required in the regulation.
observations.managementAction: GSO-Designate conducted a complete physical inventory of PPE of the Municipality with the help of a Job Order which was accomplished until December 2019. RPCPPE was accomplished based on the prescribed format in the regulation and reconciled with the balance in the Accounting Office. Each department was also furnished a copy of RPCPPE to identify the PPE under their accountability.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to exert efforts in providing the said accounts and coordinate with the affected agencies to determine measures on how they will be able to remit the said balances.
observations.managementAction: The unaccounted balance of Due to BIR amounting to P42,324.29 was remitted to BIR on December 20, 2019 thru EFPS. The unaccounted balance of Due to Phil Health account amounting to P5,434.14 was reverted to the Unappropriated Surplus account per SB Resolution No. 2019-048 dated June 6, 2019 subject to re-appropriation in case of future claims.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GSO/Appraisal and Disposal Committee to prepare the IIRUP and to facilitate the appraisal and disposal of the unserviceable properties in accordance with Section 79 of P.D. No. 1445 to avoid further deterioration and earn at least the salvage value from the sale of these assets.
observations.managementAction: The GSO-Designate explained that Appraisal and Disposal Committee was able to facilitate the appraisal and disposal of unserviceable properties with no ownership issues through public auction in CY 2020. He further stated that other unserviceable properties where legal document regarding ownership were not obtained by the Municipality causes the delay on its appraisal and disposal.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that all cash advances are liquidated at year-end in accordance with the aforementioned regulation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer has no outstanding cash advance as of December 31, 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the said personnel to coordinate with the developer in retrieving the files and data and to have the said website operational and active.
observations.managementAction: The designated IT personnel of LGU Perez was able to retrieve the files from the service provider and is currently LGU Perez is using the website perezquezon.atwebpages.com. Migrating to a government portal perezquezon.gov.ph is in process pending the approval of Department of Information and Communications Technology (DICT).

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit to the account of the National Treasury the amount of unaccounted dormant Due to NGAs accounts in the General and Trust Funds without details/breakdown.
observations.managementAction: The Sangguning Bayan of Perez passed a Resolution No. 2019-048 dated June 6, 2019 approving the reversion of the dormant Due to NGAs amounting to P2,299,261.81 to the Unappropriated Surplus account subject to re-appropriation in case of future claims.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC-MDRRMO and the Chairman and Members of the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to prioritize and optimally utilize the LDRRMF by seeking bonafide suppliers and bidders with good track record to join the LGU bidding process to strengthen the capacity of the Municipality for disaster risk reduction and management.
observations.managementAction: The BAC conducted a weekly meeting to fast-track procurement of the LDRRMF by seeking suppliers and/or bidders in neighboring Municipalities to identify an additional bonafide suppliers or bidders on similar projects that the LGU is procuring. However, the LGU is not able to finish the procurement process resulting to non-implementation of several projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality, through the Municipal Mayor, require the MPDC to expedite the collection of sex-disaggregated data that will aid in creating a GAD Database that will assist the gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The MPDC was able to come-up with a GAD Database established however, collection of sex disaggregated data was still in-process which was the baseline for LGU GAD database. (Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 11, Page No. 41)

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality, through the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board, work for the completion of their 10-year SWMP and secure the approval of the plan from the NSWMC. Also, we recommended to expedite the closure of their open dumpsite pursuant to RA No. 9003.
observations.managementAction: The MENRO-Designate scheduled a meeting with Municipal Solid Waste Management Board to discuss preparation, completion and approval of its 10-Year Solid Waste Management Plan. The conversion of Open Dumpsite to controlled Dumpsite is implemented by purchasing shredding machine. Construction of Safe Closure and Rehabilitation of Open Dumpsite was implemented by 70.83 percent completed based on the completion rate as of December 6, 2019. (Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 15, Page No. 51)

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to exert efforts in providing the details/breakdown of the said accounts and coordinate with the affected agencies to determine measures on how they will be able to remit the said balances.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant mentioned that they are hesitant to remit the said amount to the National Treasury due to the possibility that the said balance is a result of misrecording. She further mentioned that prior reconciliation disclosed that rentals due to the Municipality are included in the said accounts. As to date, confirmation from National Government Agencies (NGAs) disclosed that there are no unliquidated amounts recorded in their books pertaining to the said balance. With these being considered, she requested from the Sangguning Bayan (SB) to make a resolution to revert the said balance to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund, subject to the provision that in the event of future claims from NGAs will arise, an appropriation will be provided to which the SB agreed.
observations.reason: Despite all efforts exerted in CY 2018, the Municipal Accountant was still not able to provide the details/breakdown of the said accounts. However, she still committed to continue her efforts to comply with the audit recommendation. If the reconciliation of the accounts still proved to be futile in CY 2019, she will remit the Due to BIR account to the National Treasury and will request for the reversion of the Due to PhilHealth account to the Unappropriated Surplus of the GF, subject to an authority from the SB through a Resolution with a condition that appropriation shall be made in the event that future claims arises in the future. (Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 2, Page No. 31)

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit to the account of the National Treasury the amount of P2,299,261.81 representing the dormant Due to NGAs accounts in the General and Trust Funds without details/breakdown.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant mentioned that they are hesitant to remit the said amount to the National Treasury due to the possibility that the said balance is a result of misrecording. She further mentioned that prior reconciliation disclosed that rentals due to the Municipality are included in the said accounts. As to date, confirmation from National Government Agencies (NGAs) disclosed that there are no unliquidated amounts recorded in their books pertaining to the said balance. With these being considered, she requested from the Sangguning Bayan (SB) to make a resolution to revert the said balance to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund, subject to the provision that in the event of future claims from NGAs will arise, an appropriation will be provided to which the SB agreed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Designated General Services Officer (GSO) to prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) and to facilitate the disposal of the unserviceable properties in accordance with Section 79 of PD No. 1445 to avoid further deterioration and earn at least the salvage value from the sale of these assets.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: Disposal of the unserviceable properties was not made in CY 2018 due to conflicting schedules of the personnel assigned in the task.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to coordinate with the Sangguniang Bayan in enacting an ordinance that will govern the CFLC’s operations. We also reiterated our recommendation that the Municipality extend all assistance needed in its initial operation to attain the objectives of the project for the benefit of the constituents.
observations.managementAction: The ordinance that will govern the operation of the CFLC is still not approved in CY 2018 and is currently on its second reading.
observations.reason: The ordinance that will govern the operation of the CFLC was still not approved in CY 2018 and is currently on its second reading.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the General Services Officer to
determine the value of all the PPE items in the RPCPPE and present the said Report
per type of PPE as required in the said regulation.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant to exert efforts in providing the details/breakdown of the said accounts
and coordinate with the affected agencies to determine measures on how they will
be able to remit the said balances.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to oblige the Municipal Treasurer and the
Municipal Budget Officer to secure all the requirements in determining the insurable
amounts of the said properties and to see to it that all the insurable properties of
the Municipality are covered with insurance with the GSIS.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor coordinate with the Sangguniang Bayan
in expediting the enactment of the ordinance that will govern the CFLC’s
operations. We also reiterated our recommendation that the Municipality extend all
assistance needed in its initial operation to attain the objectives of the project for
the benefit of the constituents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GSO/Appraisal and Disposal
Committee to prepare the IIRUP and to facilitate the appraisal and disposal of the
unserviceable properties in accordance with Section 79 of PD No. 1445 to avoid
further deterioration and earn at least the salvage value from the sale of these
assets.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Assessor to continue in exerting efforts in securing all requirements/documents
needed for the titling of the said lots in the name of the Municipality to avoid third
party claims in the future and possible land disputes.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to ensure
that all cash advances are liquidated at year-end in accordance with the
aforementioned regulation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the said personnel to coordinate
with the developer in retrieving the files and data and to have the said website
operational and active.
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to exert efforts in providing the said Guaranty/ Security Deposits Payable.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant was not able to provide the details/breakdown of the said balance despite exerting all efforts. Instead, it was determined in CY 2018 that the balance may no longer represent valid claims, thus a request was submitted to the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) seeking authority in reverting the balance to the Unappropriated Surplus subject to reappropriation in the event that claims arises in the future. The said request was granted by the SB per SB Resolution No. 2018-97 dated December 7, 2018 and adjustments were made in the books of accounts thereafter.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to send written notification to the payees or holder of the checks issued one month prior to the date when issued checks will become stale so that concerned payees will be aware of the depository bank’s action on these issued checks six months after the date of its issuance. We also reiterated our recommendation to require the Municipal Accountant to make necessary adjustments in the affected accounts.
observations.managementAction: Notices were already sent to the payees or holder of the checks issued. The staled checks were already adjusted to Accounts Payable in the books of accounts.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to confirm with account owners/creditors and to request them to submit documents pertaining to their claims to the Municipality. In the event that they fail to support their claims, revert the account to the Unappropriated Surplus of the GF subject to an authority from the SB through a Resolution with a condition that appropriation shall be made in the event that future claims arises.
observations.managementAction: The said accounts were already reverted to the Unappropriated Surplus of the GF with condition that appropriation shall be made in the event that future claims arises through the authority granted by the SB per SB Resolution No. 2018-97 dated December 7, 2018. Adjustments were also made in the books of accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to trace the records which pertain to the collections of SET in CY 2010 and the collection and transfer in CY 2011 and prepare the necessary journal entries to adjust the affected accounts.
observations.managementAction: The balance was determined to be an erroneous entry and was already adjusted in the books of accounts.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor oblige the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Budget Officer to see to it that all the insurable properties of the Municipality are covered with insurance with the GSIS.
observations.managementAction: Insurance were already provided for newly acquired service and rescue vehicles. Application form for the insurance of buildings were already secured from the GSIS.
observations.reason: Requirements to determine the insurable amounts of the insurable properties were still being prepared/gathered in CY 2018.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipality, through the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board, secure the NSWMC’s approval of their 10-year SWMP and work for the conversion of their open dump to controlled dump and eventually its closure pursuant to RA No. 9003. We also recommended to implement the environmental projects of the Municipality funded from the 20 percent Development Fund to achieve the goals, objectives and intent of the said Act.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality, through the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board, secure the NSWMC’s approval of their 10-year SWMP and work for the conversion of their open dump to controlled dump and eventually its closure pursuant to RA No. 9003. We also recommended to implement the environmental projects of the Municipality funded from the 20 percent Development Fund to achieve the goals, objectives and intent of the said Act.
observations.reason: The drafting of the SWMP was still on-going as of CY 2018. Likewise, the closure of the dumpsite was still on-going.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor to continue in exerting efforts in securing all requirements/documents needed for the titling of the said lots in the name of the Municipality to avoid third party claims in the future and possible land disputes.
observations.managementAction: The certificates of title for the lots of Rural Health Unit, Gymnasium and Municipal Building were already secured.
observations.reason: The titling of the remaining lots were still in process as of year-end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor request authority from the SB for the write-off of the said receivables following the guidelines under COA Circular No. 2016-005.
observations.managementAction: Authority for write-off was already secured from SB per SB Resolution No. 2018-97.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to demand all the Municipal Collectors to submit the RCDs and remit their collections for all official receipts on a daily basis.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer required the Municipal Collectors to submit the RCDs and remit their collections on a daily basis. Collections were already remitted by the Municipal Collectors on a daily basis.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to expand the hiring of technical personnel beyond the locality or train an existing personnel for the task. We also recommended to consider the inclusion in the renewal of the web-hosting the maintenance of the website, should difficulty in hiring or training of personnel occur.
observations.managementAction: A personnel who will maintain the website was already hired in CY 2018. There is need to coordinate with the developer of the website to retrieve any existing files and data to make the website operational and active.
observations.reason: There is a need to coordinate with the developer of the website to retrieve files/data to make the website operational and active.

observations.reason:
The RPCPPE submitted is incomplete/lacking information (e.g. Unit of Measure, Unit Value, Balance per Card and On Hand, Shortage/Overage) due to absence of property/ledger cards. Please refer to Observation No. 1, page 34.

Lack of personnel and time constraints in the preparation of the ledger cards and property cards, hence reconciliation was not facilitated.

The Municipal Accountant will still ask for the Authority of the Sangguniang Bayan prior to the deposit of the Due to NGAs amount to the account of the National Treasury. Please refer to Observation No. 6, page 42.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:

require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit the amount of P1,047,367.99 to the account of the National Treasury in order to provide the national government of additional funds for other    vital    projects/
programs. 

instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit to the Auditor the supporting documents relative to the adjustments made for further evaluation.
observations.reason:
The Municipal Accountant will still ask for the Authority of the Sangguniang Bayan prior to the deposit of the afore-cited Due to NGAs amount to the account of the National Treasury. Please refer to Observation No. 6, page 42.

The Municipal Accountant is still collating the supporting documents of the adjustments for submission to the Auditor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the contractor to submit sufficient bond/guarantee, as required to avoid suspension in audit.
observations.reason: According to the Municipal Accountant, the submission of the bond/guarantee may no longer be necessary due to the full recoupment of the advance payment from the progress billings of the contractor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor create a Committee to undertake the immediate disposal of the unserviceable properties.
observations.reason:
The created Property Disposal Committee failed to convene for CY 2016 due to conflicting schedules of the officials involved.

The newly designated General Services Officer (GSO) and Inventory Committee will complete first the physical inventory of all assets to update the list of unserviceable properties prior to its disposal.

observations.managementAction: The BAC already started to procure medicines from bonafide suppliers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the LDRRMO to submit the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF (duly certified by the Municipal Accountant) to the Auditor to facilitate the timely audit on whether the utilization of the fund is in accordance with COA Memorandum No. 2014-009.
observations.reason: Monthly utilization report was submitted by the newly appointed OIC-MDRRMO to the Office of Civil Defense but was not aware that the report is also required to be submitted to the Auditor. Please refer to Observation No. 12, page 49.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Inventory Committee to complete the necessary information in the RPCPPE. Moreover, require the Municipal Accountant and Designated General Services Officer (GSO) to prepare and maintain the corresponding ledger cards and property cards for all types of properties and ensure the reconciliation of these records to establish the correct PPE balances and investigate/adjust any discrepancy that will be noted.
We further recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant to exert efforts in establishing the details/breakdown of the Other Property, Plant and Equipment of P1,291,435.49.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the Bail Bonds Payable of P1,105,304.61 to Guaranty/Security Deposits Payable and to exert efforts in providing the details/breakdown of the recorded P1,044,092.91 and P23,322.10 payable accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the accounts and remit whatever balance due to the agencies concerned to avoid incurrence of penalties and surcharges and enable the member beneficiaries of the Municipality to avail the benefits from the said offices and the government the immediate use of the funds for various programs and projects.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to notify the payees or holder of checks issued one (1) month prior to the date when issued checks will become stale so that concerned payees will be aware of the depository bank’s action on these issued checks six months after the date of its issuance.
We also recommended to require the Municipal Accountant to adjust the books of the affected accounts whenever stale checks are discovered particularly during the preparation of Bank Reconciliation Statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to confirm with account owners/creditors and to request them to submit documents pertaining to their claims to the Municipality.  In the event that they fail to support their claims, revert the account to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund, subject to an authority from the Sangguniang Bayan through a Resolution with a condition that appropriation shall be made in the event that claim arises in the future, pursuant to the provisions of Section 98 of P.D. No. 1445.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit to the account of the National Treasury the amount of P2,338,668.73 representing dormant Due to NGAs accounts in the General and Trust Funds without details/breakdown.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor to make the necessary follow-ups and continue to exert efforts in securing all requirements/documents needed for the titling of the said lots in the name of the Municipality to avoid third party claims in the future and possible land disputes.

We recommended the full utilization of the 20% Development Fund through the implementation of the projects that will contribute to the development of the Municipality which will be beneficial to its constituents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Treasurer see to it that all the insurable properties of the Municipality are covered with insurance with the GSIS in compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 92-390 and RA No. 656.

We recommended that the Municipality, through the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board, secure the NSWMC’s approval of their 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan and work for the conversion of open dump to controlled dump and its closure pursuant to R.A. No. 9003 and implement the environmental project under the 20% Development Fund to achieve the goals, objectives and intent of the said Act.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate one percent (1%) of IRA for LCPC programs, projects and activities for the protection of children as required under DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with the requirements stated under COA Circular No. 92-389 to prevent the possible re-use of those documents for other claims of same nature as part of a sound internal control system in the disbursement of funds.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the MDRRMO to submit to the Audit Team the Monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRMF on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC and the LDC in compliance with Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular 2012-002.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:
instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical inventory and prepare and submit the RPCPPE per type of property.








require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to prepare the corresponding ledger cards and property cards for all types of properties and reconcile the same to establish the correct PPE balances. Any discrepancy that may be noted be investigated and adjusted accordingly.
observations.managementAction: The Inventory Committee conducted the physical inventory and prepared the RPCPPE per type of property.
The Municipal Accountant has already made the reversal/ adjusting entry.

The Municipal Assessor already made several follow-ups on the status of the approval of the application for Special Patent.





The Municipal Assessor has likewise applied for the Special Patent of two of the other three untitled lots mentioned in CY 2015 AAR.

observations.reason:
Despite the follow-ups made by the Municipal Assessor, the titling of the lots in the name of the Municipality did not prosper due to the required Certification from the Land Registration Authority (LRA) of No Pending Land Registration. Please refer to Observation No. 7, page 43.

The same reason as stated above.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to strictly adhere with the regulations and deposit all municipal collections promptly and intact in order to protect municipal funds against risk of loss and possible misuse.
observations.managementAction: Deposits were already made intact on a weekly basis by the Municipal Treasurer.
observations.reason: As mentioned in the exit conference, it would be impractical for the Municipal Treasurer (MT) to deposit collections strictly on a daily basis. The travel period from the Municipality to the depository bank and vice versa usually consumes almost two working days. Doing so, would mean that the MT would have to sacrifice all her other regular duties and functions in the Municipality and would also entail a lot of travelling costs for the Municipality. Nevertheless, the MT sees to it that all collections from the previous week are deposited intact the following week.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Collectors and Municipal Treasurer to properly accomplish, verify, and sign the RCDs for proper control and monitoring of collections and deposits.
observations.managementAction:
The RCDs were already properly accomplished, signed by the Collectors and verified by the 
Municipal Treasurer.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer/MSWDO to send   collection      notices/
demand letters and exert all efforts to collect the said receivables from the concerned beneficiaries to ensure the continuity of the livelihood            programs/
projects and give other prospective beneficiaries equal opportunity in availing capital/livelihood assistance. If all means failed, resort to legal action to avoid loss of government funds.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer and MSWDO sent collection notices/demand letters in May 2016.
observations.reason: Due to busy schedule, monitoring of the movement of the receivables, necessary follow-ups and reminders to the beneficiaries of their obligations were not given priority.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to settle immediately her outstanding cash advance. Otherwise, effect the withholding of her salary in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-002.
observations.managementAction: The aforementioned cash advances were already liquidated as of year-end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to remit the barangay share from RPT and CTC within the prescribed period in compliance with the provisions of Section 271(d) of R.A. No. 7160, in order to provide for the immediate use of the funds for various projects/programs of the Barangays.
observations.managementAction: Remittances of the Barangay Shares are now being made on a quarterly basis.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor submit the:

list of all on-going government    projects/
programs/activities (“PPAs”) and those that are to be implemented during the year, as of January 1, 2015; and
monthly monitoring report in compliance with the above cited regulations.
observations.managementAction:
The list of PPAs was submitted to the Audit Team.




The monthly monitoring report was submitted to the Auditor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Chairman or Secretariat to submit copies of perfected contracts and POs together with the supporting documents within five working days from issuance thereof to facilitate the conduct of auditorial and technical review, in accordance with COA Circular No. 2009-001.
observations.managementAction: Copies of the perfected contracts were already submitted to the Auditor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Bids and Awards Committee to also send invitation to at least one (1) observer from a duly recognized private group/sector in all stages of the procurement in accordance with the above cited regulations.
observations.managementAction: The BAC is already sending invitation to observer from the private sector.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: 

Bids and Awards Committee and Municipal Accountant to ensure that all purchases of drugs and medicines are supported with the required certification by the requisitioning officer or by the duly authorized officer to have that assurance, that at the time of the purchase request, the drugs and medicines were included in the latest PNF Manual as mandated by DOH Administrative Order No. 2012-0023.

Municipal Health Officer/Requisitioning Officer to submit to the Auditor the required certification, for evaluation.
observations.managementAction:
Requisition of medicines is already supported with the Certification from the Municipal Health Officer that the medicines are included in the latest PNF Manual.






The required certification was already submitted to the Auditor.

observations.reason: The utilization of the Trust Fund – Philhealth was still not maximized due to failure of the Municipal Health Officer to prepare detailed Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP). Please refer to Observation No. 15, page 52.

observations.managementAction:
A written explanation was submitted by the BAC Chairman. However, the said transaction was disallowed in audit for failure to require/secure a valid/updated tax clearance at the time of bidding.



The members of BAC are now verifying all documents submitted by bidders to ensure that they are eligible prior to the award of the project/contract.
